message:
feat(demo_showcase): implement scrollable help overlay with canonical keymap

- Add HelpEntry and HelpSection structs to keymap.rs for structured help content
- Define HELP_SECTIONS with Global, Navigation, Selection, Search, and Mouse categories
- Add help_scroll_offset field to App for scrollable help content
- Implement handle_help_key() for vim-style navigation (j/k/g/G/Ctrl-d/Ctrl-u)
- Enhance render_help() to use keymap data with section headers and page context
- Help overlay now shows current page hints and scroll position indicator
- Supports narrow terminals with truncation and percentage scroll indicator

Closes bd-1aad

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-194c","title":"demo_showcase core: shell-out action (release/restore terminal + Cmd::sequence demo)","description":"Add a *showcase-grade* example of temporarily giving the user their terminal back, running a real command, then restoring the TUI.

Why this matters:
- This is a signature Bubble Tea capability ("drop to shell" / run external tools) that makes TUIs feel like real products.
- It exercises bubbletea’s terminal lifecycle helpers and demonstrates *how to do side effects correctly*.
- It provides a practical escape hatch for users: open a pager, dump diagnostics, run a quick command, then return.

What to build:
- A reusable helper that returns a bubbletea `Cmd` using `bubbletea::sequence(...)` to:
  1) `bubbletea::screen::release_terminal()` (restore cooked mode, show cursor, leave alt-screen if enabled)
  2) run an external command (prefer `$PAGER`, fallback to a built-in "press Enter to return" prompt)
  3) `bubbletea::screen::restore_terminal()` and force a full redraw
- At least one in-app entrypoint to trigger it (pick one):
  - Command palette action (preferred if present)
  - Settings/About "Open diagnostics in pager" action
  - Logs "Open selection in pager" action

Design constraints:
- Must be deterministic and CI-safe:
  - In `--self-check` / custom-IO mode, the action must NOT hang waiting for a pager.
  - In headless mode, it should instead log what *would* have happened and no-op.
- Avoid platform brittleness:
  - If spawning a pager, keep it optional and gracefully handle missing `$PAGER` / non-zero exit.

Acceptance:
- Triggering the action never leaves the terminal broken (cursor, raw mode, alt screen all restored).
- The implementation uses `bubbletea::sequence` (no ad-hoc sleeps; no manual "state machine" for this flow).
- Failure modes are user-friendly: errors become toasts/logs rather than panics.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:49.242205979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:33.088610036Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-194c","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:33.088580151Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-194c","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:31.777567228Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-194c","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:30.460167582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-194c","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:49.273219579Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1aad","title":"demo_showcase core: help overlay + key hints","description":"Implement an in-app Help overlay that makes the demo self-teaching.

Why:
- A flagship demo must be usable without reading source.
- Help is also where we can advertise advanced features (mouse, focus, paste, command palette).

Requirements:
- Trigger:
  - `?` toggles the overlay
  - `esc` closes
- Content:
  - global keybindings (navigation, quit, help, focus, theme)
  - current page keybindings (contextual section)
  - small notes for mouse + paste behavior (if enabled)
- Presentation:
  - use `bubbles::help` and/or a custom lipgloss-rendered modal
  - scrollable if content exceeds height
  - looks like part of the product, not a debug dump

Integration:
- Source the keymap from the canonical UX keybindings definition so the overlay cannot drift.

Acceptance:
- Easy to extend as new features/pages are added.
- Help overlay never breaks layout and works in narrow terminals.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:59.917442045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:27:32.677327397Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1aad","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:21.843105299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1aad","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:20.649419393Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1aad","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:59.930408930Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1aad","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:23.042384073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3chy","title":"demo_showcase: optional SSH server mode (wish)","description":"Add an optional mode that serves the showcase over SSH (via `wish`) to prove the remote/SSH story is viable.

Why:
- Wish is one of the Charm ecosystem’s most compelling differentiators.
- Serving the same UI over SSH demonstrates composability: bubbletea Program IO abstraction + wish sessions + terminal capability negotiation.

Requirements:
- CLI:
  - `demo_showcase --ssh <addr>` (e.g., `:2222` or `0.0.0.0:2222`) starts the server.
  - host key UX:
    - accept `--host-key <path>` and/or generate a key if missing (decide in child tasks)
- Session behavior:
  - each SSH connection gets its own app instance (or a curated subset if full UI is too heavy)
  - wire bubbletea input/output to the SSH session streams
  - handle PTY sizing and resize events (best-effort)
- Security/ergonomics:
  - basic auth/middleware polish (simple but not embarrassing)
  - clear logs + startup banner (address, auth mode, host key path)

Acceptance:
- Running the server and connecting with `ssh -p 2222 localhost` launches the UI and is usable.
- Works with minimal setup beyond a host key.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - argument parsing and config wiring for ssh mode
  - host-key path validation logic
- E2E scripts:
  - a CI-safe “handshake + banner” test (non-interactive) that proves the server starts and accepts connections.
  - a local “interactive smoke” script (optional) that drives basic navigation via SSH and captures logs/artifacts.

Notes:
- Keep this optional: the main value of demo_showcase should not depend on SSH being enabled.
- Be realistic about CI environments; gate the heavier SSH tests appropriately.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.593755071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:44:44.471178493Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ssh","wish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3chy","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.612205255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3d1w","title":"Dashboard: mouse affordances (clickable cards)","description":"Add mouse affordances to the Dashboard so it feels like a modern interactive console.

Why:
- Mouse support is an "advanced" feature that many terminal apps skip.
- When done well (subtle highlights + click actions), it makes the demo feel shockingly polished.

Behaviors:
- Click to select/focus a dashboard card/widget.
- Click on obvious affordances (e.g., "View logs" / "Open job") to navigate.
- Hover/selection highlight (keyboard focus and mouse selection should be visually consistent).

Constraints:
- Must degrade gracefully when mouse is disabled (Config toggle) or terminal doesn’t support it.
- Wheel scroll should not break layout; only scroll the active viewport/list when applicable.

Acceptance:
- Click selects a card and visibly focuses it.
- Mouse interactions never steal focus unexpectedly or cause layout corruption.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:01.759013697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:44.024189348Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:25.679670153Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:01.778296349Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:24.421135419Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3d69","title":"bubbletea: Add debug logging for channel disconnection","description":"# Task: Add Debug Logging for Channel Disconnection

## Problem Statement
Throughout the event loop, channel send errors are silently discarded:

```rust
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);  // Error ignored
```

When the main thread exits (panic, quit, etc.), spawned command threads
continue executing but their results disappear into the void. There's no
logging or visibility into these "orphaned" command executions.

## Affected Locations
- Line 610: External message forwarding
- Line 628: Custom input parser
- Line 680-690: Event messages
- Line 838: Batch command results
- Line 847: Sequence command results
- Line 852: Normal command results

## Impact
- **Debugging difficulty**: Hard to diagnose when commands execute but effects don't appear
- **Resource waste**: Commands continue executing after they're no longer needed
- **Silent failures**: No indication that something went wrong

## Solution Design

### Use tracing for Debug Logging
```rust
use tracing::{debug, warn};

// Replace:
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);

// With:
if let Err(e) = tx_clone.send(msg) {
    debug!(
        target: "bubbletea::command",
        error = %e,
        "Command result dropped - receiver disconnected"
    );
}
```

### Log Levels
- **debug**: Normal disconnection during shutdown (expected)
- **warn**: Disconnection during active event loop (unexpected)

### Context Information
Include useful context in log messages:
- Command type (if available)
- Whether this is during shutdown
- Thread/task ID

## Implementation Steps
1. Add tracing as dependency (already present for bubbletea)
2. Replace all `let _ = tx.send()` with logged version
3. Add context about shutdown state where available
4. Add example showing how to capture these logs
5. Document the logging behavior

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn channel_disconnection_logged() {
    // Set up tracing subscriber that captures logs
    let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel();
    drop(rx);  // Disconnect receiver
    
    // This should log at debug level
    if let Err(e) = tx.send(Message::new(42)) {
        // Verify error type
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All channel send failures logged at debug level
- [ ] Logs include useful context (location, state)
- [ ] No performance impact when logging disabled
- [ ] Example in docs showing how to capture logs
- [ ] No change to public API","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.262130512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.289839054Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","error-handling","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d69","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.289804229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3d69","title":"bubbletea: Add debug logging for channel disconnection","description":"# Task: Add Debug Logging for Channel Disconnection

## Problem Statement
Throughout the event loop, channel send errors are silently discarded:

\`\`\`rust
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);  // Error ignored
\`\`\`

When the main thread exits (panic, quit, etc.), spawned command threads
continue executing but their results disappear into the void. There's no
logging or visibility into these "orphaned" command executions.

## Affected Locations
- Line 610: External message forwarding
- Line 628: Custom input parser
- Line 680-690: Event messages
- Line 838: Batch command results
- Line 847: Sequence command results
- Line 852: Normal command results

## Impact
- **Debugging difficulty**: Hard to diagnose when commands execute but effects don't appear
- **Resource waste**: Commands continue executing after they're no longer needed
- **Silent failures**: No indication that something went wrong

## Solution Design

### Use tracing for Debug Logging
\`\`\`rust
use tracing::{debug, warn, instrument};

// Replace:
let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);

// With:
if let Err(e) = tx_clone.send(msg) {
    debug!(
        target: "bubbletea::command",
        error = %e,
        command_type = ?cmd_type,
        "Command result dropped - receiver disconnected"
    );
}
\`\`\`

### Structured Logging Fields
\`\`\`rust
struct CommandContext {
    command_id: u64,
    command_type: &'static str,
    batch_id: Option<u64>,
}

// Log with context
debug!(
    target: "bubbletea",
    command_id = ctx.command_id,
    command_type = ctx.command_type,
    batch_id = ?ctx.batch_id,
    "Command result dropped"
);
\`\`\`

### Log Levels
- **trace**: All channel sends (very verbose, for deep debugging)
- **debug**: Failed sends during normal operation
- **warn**: Failed sends that indicate unexpected state

## Implementation Steps
1. Add tracing feature flag (already present)
2. Create helper function for logged send
3. Replace all \`let _ = tx.send()\` with logged version
4. Add command context tracking where feasible
5. Add documentation example
6. Add integration test for log capture

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
use tracing_test::traced_test;

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn channel_disconnection_is_logged() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    drop(rx);  // Disconnect receiver
    
    // This should log at debug level
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_err());
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result dropped"));
    assert!(logs_contain("test_command"));
}

#[traced_test]
#[test]
fn successful_send_logs_at_trace_level() {
    let (tx, rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel::<Message>();
    
    let result = logged_send(&tx, Message::new(42), "test_command");
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    // Trace level logs successful sends
    assert!(logs_contain("Command result sent"));
}
\`\`\`

### Integration Test
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn logs_include_command_context() {
    use tracing_subscriber::layer::SubscriberExt;
    
    let (captured, guard) = setup_log_capture();
    
    let program = Program::new(TestModel);
    let cmd = Command::new(|| {
        std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));
        Some(Message::new("delayed"))
    });
    
    program.send(cmd);
    program.quit();  // Quit before command completes
    
    // Command result should be logged as dropped
    let logs = captured.into_inner();
    assert!(logs.iter().any(|l| l.contains("dropped")));
}
\`\`\`

### E2E Test Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# Test: bubbletea_logging_e2e.sh

set -e
echo "=== bubbletea Channel Logging Tests ==="

# Enable trace logging
export RUST_LOG=bubbletea=debug

# Run tests with log capture
cargo test --package bubbletea channel_disconnection -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log

# Verify expected log messages appear
if grep -q "Command result dropped" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Channel disconnection logging works"
else
    echo "[FAIL] Channel disconnection not logged"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify structured fields present
if grep -q "command_type" /tmp/bubbletea_logging.log; then
    echo "[PASS] Structured logging fields present"
else
    echo "[WARN] Missing structured fields - check log format"
fi

echo "=== Test Complete ==="
\`\`\`

## Documentation Example
\`\`\`rust
//! # Debugging bubbletea Command Execution
//!
//! Enable debug logging to see command execution flow:
//!
//! \`\`\`bash
//! RUST_LOG=bubbletea=debug cargo run
//! \`\`\`
//!
//! For even more detail:
//! \`\`\`bash
//! RUST_LOG=bubbletea=trace cargo run
//! \`\`\`
//!
//! ## Log Message Reference
//!
//! | Level | Message | Meaning |
//! |-------|---------|---------|
//! | trace | "Command result sent" | Normal operation |
//! | debug | "Command result dropped" | Receiver disconnected |
//! | warn | "Command failed during active loop" | Unexpected |
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All channel send failures logged at debug level
- [ ] Logs include command context (type, id)
- [ ] Structured logging with tracing
- [ ] No performance impact when logging disabled
- [ ] Unit tests with tracing_test
- [ ] Integration test for log capture
- [ ] E2E test script validates logging
- [ ] Documentation example added","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.262130512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:33:29.920025821Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","error-handling","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d69","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:50.289804229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3d87","title":"Docs: syntax highlighting + line numbers toggle","description":"Add syntax highlighting and optional line numbers to rendered Markdown in the Docs page.

Why:
- Syntax highlighting is a flagship Glamour feature and a major "wow" factor.
- It also demonstrates that the demo respects runtime toggles and terminal capabilities.

Requirements:
- Highlight fenced code blocks when the feature is enabled.
- Optional line numbers (toggle-able):
  - consistent width/padding
  - do not break wrapping
- Must respect:
  - no-color / ASCII mode (disable highlighting cleanly)
  - Config/Settings toggles (syntax on/off, line numbers on/off)

Performance:
- Avoid re-highlighting on every keystroke; only re-render on relevant changes (doc switch, resize, theme/toggles).

Acceptance:
- Code blocks look great and readable.

 {"id":"bd-3ecr","title":"E2E scenario: settings toggles + theme switching","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that validates Settings actually changes the running app.

Scenario outline (headless/custom-IO):
1) Launch demo_showcase in a deterministic mode (fixed seed, animations off).
2) Navigate to Settings.
3) Toggle at least:
   - no-color / ASCII mode (or equivalent)
   - mouse enable (even if headless, ensure toggle state updates)
4) Switch theme preset and confirm global restyling takes effect.
5) Return to Dashboard (or another page) and confirm the changes persist across navigation.

Assertions:
- Output changes in expected ways (e.g., theme name label updates, ANSI sequences absent when no-color).
- No panics and no hangs.

Acceptance:
- Scenario runs deterministically in CI.
- On failure, the artifact bundle clearly shows the last page and the toggle/theme state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.112857002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.150108808Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.145700883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.141257332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.150092478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.137009755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.131740545Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3eru","title":"Unit tests: filtering engines (jobs/logs/docs)","description":"Add focused unit tests for filtering/search logic (without spinning up the full UI).

Why:
- Filtering and search are easy to subtly break (case sensitivity, tokenization, pagination interactions).
- We want fast feedback during refactors.

Coverage:
- Jobs filtering/sorting:
  - query matches by name/id/service
  - status filters compose correctly
  - sorting is stable and deterministic
- Logs filtering:
  - level/service selectors work
  - free-text query matches expected fields
- Docs search:
  - match finding and next/prev navigation over a rendered buffer

Edge cases to include:
- empty query
- Unicode input
- very long query (no panic, bounded behavior)
- paste-like input containing newlines/whitespace

Nice-to-have:
- Add a small set of property tests (e.g., filter is idempotent, sorting preserves multiset) if it stays lightweight.

Acceptance:
- Core filter logic is correct and stays correct during refactors.
- Tests are deterministic and run fast.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.169792772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:27.066580206Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-1fxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:26.759858058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-212j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:25.449597433Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:28.296789159Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.182594415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+// ============================================================================
+// Help Overlay Content Generation
+// ============================================================================
+
+/// A single keybinding entry for help display.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
+pub struct HelpEntry {
+    /// The key combination (e.g., "j/↓", "Ctrl+C").
+    pub key: &'static str,
+    /// The action description (e.g., "Move down").
+    pub action: &'static str,
+}
+
+impl HelpEntry {
+    /// Create a new help entry.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn new(key: &'static str, action: &'static str) -> Self {
+        Self { key, action }
+    }
+}
+
+/// A section of keybindings for help display.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
+pub struct HelpSection {
+    /// Section title (e.g., "Global", "Navigation").
+    pub title: &'static str,
+    /// Keybinding entries in this section.
+    pub entries: &'static [HelpEntry],
+}
+
+/// Global keybindings section.
+pub const HELP_GLOBAL: HelpSection = HelpSection {
+    title: "Global",
+    entries: &[
+        HelpEntry::new("?", "Toggle this help"),
+        HelpEntry::new("q / Esc", "Quit application"),
+        HelpEntry::new("Ctrl+C", "Force quit"),
+        HelpEntry::new("1-7", "Jump to page"),
+        HelpEntry::new("[", "Toggle sidebar"),
+        HelpEntry::new("t", "Cycle theme"),
+        HelpEntry::new("Tab", "Focus next pane"),
+        HelpEntry::new("S-Tab", "Focus previous pane"),
+    ],
+};
+
+/// Navigation keybindings section.
+pub const HELP_NAVIGATION: HelpSection = HelpSection {
+    title: "Navigation",
+    entries: &[
+        HelpEntry::new("j / ↓", "Move down"),
+        HelpEntry::new("k / ↑", "Move up"),
+        HelpEntry::new("h / ←", "Left / collapse"),
+        HelpEntry::new("l / →", "Right / expand"),
+        HelpEntry::new("g / Home", "Go to top"),
+        HelpEntry::new("G / End", "Go to bottom"),
+        HelpEntry::new("Ctrl+d", "Half page down"),
+        HelpEntry::new("Ctrl+u", "Half page up"),
+        HelpEntry::new("PgDn", "Page down"),
+        HelpEntry::new("PgUp", "Page up"),
+    ],
+};
+
+/// Selection and action keybindings section.
+pub const HELP_SELECTION: HelpSection = HelpSection {
+    title: "Selection & Actions",
+    entries: &[
+        HelpEntry::new("Enter", "Select / activate"),
+        HelpEntry::new("Space", "Toggle selection"),
+        HelpEntry::new("a", "Select all"),
+        HelpEntry::new("x", "Clear selection"),
+        HelpEntry::new("d", "Delete selected"),
+        HelpEntry::new("e", "Edit selected"),
+        HelpEntry::new("c", "Copy selected"),
+        HelpEntry::new("r", "Refresh view"),
+        HelpEntry::new("n", "New item"),
+    ],
+};
+
+/// Search keybindings section.
+pub const HELP_SEARCH: HelpSection = HelpSection {
+    title: "Search",
+    entries: &[
+        HelpEntry::new("/", "Open search"),
+        HelpEntry::new("n", "Next match"),
+        HelpEntry::new("N", "Previous match"),
+        HelpEntry::new("Esc", "Clear search"),
+    ],
+};
+
+/// Mouse interaction section.
+pub const HELP_MOUSE: HelpSection = HelpSection {
+    title: "Mouse (when enabled)",
+    entries: &[
+        HelpEntry::new("Click", "Focus / select"),
+        HelpEntry::new("Double-click", "Primary action"),
+        HelpEntry::new("Scroll", "Scroll viewport"),
+    ],
+};
+
+/// All help sections in display order.
+pub const HELP_SECTIONS: &[&HelpSection] = &[
+    &HELP_GLOBAL,
+    &HELP_NAVIGATION,
+    &HELP_SELECTION,
+    &HELP_SEARCH,
+    &HELP_MOUSE,
+];
+
+/// Calculate the total number of lines in the help content.
+///
+/// Each section has: title line + entries + blank line.
+#[must_use]
+pub fn help_total_lines() -> usize {
+    let mut lines = 0;
+    for section in HELP_SECTIONS {
+        lines += 1; // Section title
+        lines += section.entries.len(); // Entries
+        lines += 1; // Blank line after section
+    }
+    lines
+}
+

 mod tests {
     use super::*;
